reasoning: The question asks whether the US Federal Reserve will cut interest rates before July 1, 2025. The Fed met on May 7, 2025, and decided to hold rates steady, citing a wait-and-see approach. The only scheduled FOMC meeting before July 1, 2025, is on June 17-18. Market expectations for a June cut had been solid (above 60%) in early April but fell sharply after a strong April jobs report and uncertainties related to tariffs, which pushed expectations to July or even September. The CME FedWatch tool indicated only about a 33% probability of a June cut as of May 7-8. Furthermore, major investment banks like Goldman Sachs and Barclays have formally revised their forecasts to predict the first cut of 2025 will occur in July, not June. Given the Fed's emphasized need for more data clarity—which isn't expected until June or July—and the specific signaling holding off until at least July, a June rate cut is quite unlikely. There is roughly an 18-20% chance they make a surprise move in June if May's inflation/jobs numbers report significantly below expectations.
